summary: DHH compares the Nokia 3650 and Nokia 6600 mobile phones, arguing that the most important upgrade is the jump from 4096 to 16-bit color depth. While acknowledging the 6600's improvements in size, weight, and keyboard, he focuses on how the increased color range dramatically improves video playback quality. He demonstrates this with a comparison frame from a Simpsons episode encoded for both displays.
key_points: The Nokia 6600 offers practical improvements like smaller size and standard keyboard, but color depth matters more for video
16-bit color vs 4096 colors makes a dramatic visible difference in mobile video playback
Mobile video consumption was already a use case DHH cared about in 2003
money_quotes: What matters much more, though, is the color range.
Who cares about the amount of colors on a mobile phone? Video does. Greatly.
The difference is more than noticeable.
key_insight: Even in 2003, DHH was focused on mobile media consumption quality, arguing that color depth for video playback mattered more than form factor improvements in phone hardware.
